incident_report:
# Incident Report: Dungeon Agent Run (Seed 42)

**Summary:** This run terminated at the turn limit (turn 16 of 15 allowed) with zero progress toward objectives. Agent B encountered spurious block anomalies on turns 1, 4, 5, and 10 during movement commands, while Agent A hit the same anomaly type on turns 7 and 14. A critical cascading failure occurred on turn 14 when both agents simultaneously triggered spurious blocks, suggesting either environmental corruption or a pathfinding algorithm malfunction.

**Root Cause:** The high frequency of spurious_block anomalies (7 total across 16 turns) indicates either faulty movement validation logic or an environmental hazard that was never cleared. Neither agent managed to send messages, unlock doors, or reach key pickups, suggesting they became trapped or unable to progress from their starting positions.

**Decision Point Analysis:** The most notable failure occurred at turn 14 when both agents hit anomalies simultaneously, consuming the final viable turns before timeout. Earlier intervention after Agent B's third spurious block (turn 5) might have allowed strategy revision, but the lack of any DM interactions suggests the agents did not attempt to communicate the problem or request guidance.
